blocks:
Sūrat Al-Muṭaffifīn

And it is called Sūrat At-Taṭfīf, Makkan. And it is said to be Madinan, except for eight verses from it. And they are إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا ** until the end of the Sūrah. The number of its verses is thirty-six by consensus (بالإِجماع).

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَيْلٌ لِلْمُطَفِّفِينَ ** (1) Woe to the defrauders, Meaning, to those who diminish [the measure]. Al-Qummi said: [They are] those who diminish the measure (المكيال) and the balance (الميزان). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: It was revealed to the Prophet of Allah when He arrived in Medina, and they were, on that day, the worst people in measuring (كيلاً). Then they improved their practice of measuring (عمل الكيل) afterwards. As for al-Wayl (الويل), it has reached us—and Allah knows best—that it is a well in Hell (جهنّم). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): And it was revealed concerning measuring: وَيْلٌ لِلْمُطَفِّفِينَ ** (Woe to the defrauders). And He did not assign al-Wayl (الويل) to anyone until He named him a disbeliever (كافراً). The Almighty Allah said: فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ مَشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ. (So woe to those who disbelieve, because of the presence of a tremendous day.)

(٢) الَّذِينَ إِذَا اكْتالُوا عَلَى النَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ ** (2) those who, when they measure [a quantity] from the people, take it in full, Meaning: If they measure [a quantity] from the people—their rights—they take them fully (وافية). (٣) وَإِذا كالُوهُمْ أَوْ وَزَنُوهُمْ ** (3) but when they measure or weigh for them, they give less than due. Meaning: If they measure for the people or weigh for them, they give less than due (يُخْسِرُونَ). (٤) أَلا يَظُنُّ أُولئِكَ أَنَّهُمْ مَبْعُوثُونَ ** (4) Do not such people think that they will be resurrected Do they not have certainty (يوقنون) that they will be resurrected (مَبْعُوثُونَ)? Thus, it is narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him); it is narrated in al-Ihtijaj. (٥) لِيَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (5) for a tremendous day, He made it tremendous due to the tremendousness of what will occur therein. (٦) يَوْمَ يَقُومُ النَّاسُ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (6) the day when mankind will stand before the Lord of all the worlds? For His judgment. In al-Majma', it came in the hadith: They will stand in their sweat (رشحهم) up to the middle of their ears. And in another hadith: They will stand until the sweat (الرّشح) reaches the tips of their ears. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The likeness of the people on the Day of Resurrection when they stand before the Lord of all the worlds is like the arrow in the quiver (القراب); they have nothing of the earth except the place of their foot, like the arrow in the quiver (الكنانة); they are unable to move here or there.

(٧) كَلَّا ** (7) No, indeed! [The word ** كَلَّا ** is] a deterrent against short-changing (in measure and weight) (التّطفيف) and heedlessness (الغفلة) concerning the Resurrection (البعث) and the Reckoning (الحساب). إِنَّ كِتابَ الفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٍ. (7) Indeed, the register of the vicious is in Sijjīn. (٨) وَما أَدْراكَ ما سِجِّينٌ. (8) And what will show you what Sijjīn is? (٩) كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ ** (9) It is a marked register. Al-Qummi said: [It is] what Allah has decreed (كتب) for them of the punishment (العذاب). لَفِي سِجِّينٍ. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As-Sijjīn (السجّين) is the seventh earth, and عِلِّيُّونَ ** (ʿIlliyyūn) is the seventh heaven. And in al-Majmaʿ, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "As for the believers (المؤمنون), their deeds and their souls (أرواحهم) are raised to the heaven, and its gates are opened for them. As for the disbeliever (الكافر), he ascends with his deed and his soul until, when he reaches the heaven, a caller announces: 'Descend with him to سِجِّينٍ ,' which is a valley in Ḥaḍramawt (حضرموت) called Barhūt (برهوت)." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: إِنَّ كِتابَ الفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٍ ** (Indeed, the register of the vicious is in Sijjīn). He said: "They are those who committed wickedness (فجروا) regarding the right of the Imams (peace be upon them) and transgressed against them." And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), said: "It is so-and-so and so-and-so (فلان وفلان)."

(١٠) وَيْلٌ (١) يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (10) Woe to the deniers on that Day! (١١) الَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ بِيَوْمِ الدِّينِ ** (11) those who deny the Day of Retribution. The first and the second said. (١٢) وَما يُكَذِّبُ بِهِ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ. (12) None denies it except every transgressor, sinner. (١٣) إِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ آياتُنا قالَ أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (13) When Our signs are recited to him, he says, 'Myths of the ancients!' He said, and they are the first and the second, who used to deny the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. (١٤) كَلَّا ** (14) No indeed! A deterrent against this saying بَلْ رانَ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ.** rather, their hearts have been stained by what they used to earn.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: "There is no believing servant except that in his heart there is a white spot (نكتة بيضاء). If he commits a sin, a black spot (نكتة سوداء) emerges from that spot. If he repents, that blackness disappears. But if he persists in sins, that blackness increases until it covers the whiteness. When the whiteness is covered, its possessor will never return to goodness, and this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: كَلَّا بَلْ رانَ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ. (No indeed! rather, their hearts have been stained by what they used to earn.)"

footnote (١) And this is a threat to whoever denied the recompense and the Resurrection and did not believe.

(١٥) كَلَّا إِنَّهُمْ عَنْ رَبِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَمَحْجُوبُونَ. (15) No indeed! They will surely be veiled from their Lord on that day.

In al-Uyun and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: Indeed, Allah the Almighty is not described by a place in which He resides, such that His servants would be veiled from Him therein. But rather, it means: إِنَّهُمْ عَنْ ثَوَابِ رَبِّهِمْ لَمَحْجُوبُونَ (they will surely be veiled from the reward of their Lord).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): (It means they are veiled) from His reward and the abode of His generosity (دار كرامته).

(١٦) ثُمَّ إِنَّهُمْ لَصالُوا الْجَحِيمِ ** (16) Then they will indeed enter the Hellfire. They will enter the Fire and be burned by it (يَصْلَوْنَ بِهَا). (١٧) ثُمَّ يُقالُ هذَا الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ. (17) Then it will be said, 'This is what you used to deny.' In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: "It means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." It was asked: "Is this a revelation (تنزيل)?" He said: "Yes." (١٨) كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الْأَبْرارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ ** (18) No indeed! The Book of the Pious is indeed in ‘Illiyyun. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, what has been written for them of reward (الثواب). (١٩) وَما أَدْراكَ ما عِلِّيُّونَ. (19) And what will show you what ‘Illiyyun is? (٢٠) كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ. (20) A marked Book. (٢١) يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ. (21) Which the Hallowed ones witness. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: "Indeed, Allah created us from the highest ‘Illiyyun, and He created the hearts of our followers (شيعتنا) from that from which He created us, and He created their bodies from something lesser than that. Their hearts yearn for us because they were created from that from which we were created." Then He recited this Ayah: كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الْأَبْرارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ وَما أَدْرَاكَ ما عِلِّيُّونَ كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ (No indeed! The Book of the Pious is indeed in ‘Illiyyun. And what will show you what ‘Illiyyun is? A marked Book, Which the Hallowed ones witness.) "And He created our enemy from Sijjin, and He created the hearts of their followers (شيعتهم) from that from which He created them, and their bodies from something lesser than that. So their hearts yearn for them because they were created from that from which they were created." Then He recited this Ayah: كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٍ وَما أَدْرَاكَ ما سِجِّينٌ كِتابٌ مَرْقُومٌ وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ. (No indeed! The Book of the Vicious is indeed in Sijjin. And what will show you what Sijjin is? A marked Book. Woe to the deniers on that day!) I say: The repeated actions (الأفاعيل) and the firmly established beliefs (الاعتقادات الراسخة) in the souls are equivalent to written inscriptions (النّقوش الكتابيّة) on tablets (الألواح). Thus, whoever possesses sacred matters (أموراً قدسيّة) as his knowledge, and his character (أخلاقه) is pure, and his deeds (اعماله) are righteous, his book (كتابه) will come to him in his right hand (بيمينه), meaning from his stronger, spiritual side (الجانب الأقوى الرّوحاني), which is the direction (جهة) of ‘Illiyyun. This is because his book is of the genus of the high tablets (الألواح العالية) and the honored, elevated, purified scrolls (الصحف المكرّمة المرفوعة المطهّرة): بِأَيْدِي سَفَرَةٍ كِرامٍ بَرَرَةٍ يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ (In the hands of scribes, noble and pious, Which the Hallowed ones witness). And whoever’s knowledge is restricted to corporeal matters (الجرميّات), and his character is evil (سيّئة), and his deeds are wicked (خبيثة), his book will come to him in his left hand (بشماله), meaning from his weaker, physical side (الجانب الأضعف الجسماني), which is the direction of Sijjin. This is because his book is of the genus of the lower papers (الأوراق السّفليّة) and the sensory scrolls (الصّحائف الحسّيّة) that are susceptible to burning. Therefore, no body is tormented by the Fire; rather, it is the return of the souls to that from which they were created, just as He, glory be to Him, said: كَما بَدَأَكُمْ تَعُودُونَ (as He originated you, you will return). So, whoever was created from ‘Illiyyun, his book is in ‘Illiyyun, and whoever was created from Sijjin, his book is in Sijjin.

(٢٢) إِنَّ الْأَبْرارَ لَفِي نَعِيمٍ. (22) Indeed the pious will be amid bliss. (٢٣) عَلَى الْأَرائِكِ يَنْظُرُونَ ** (23) [seated] on couches, looking on. Upon the couches in the chambers (الحجال), they look upon what delights them of the bliss. (٢٤) تَعْرِفُ فِي وُجُوهِهِمْ نَضْرَةَ النَّعِيمِ ** (24) You will recognize in their faces the freshness of bliss. The splendor of enjoyment (التنعّم) and its radiance. And it was recited (تُعْرَفُ) (in the passive voice) and (نَضْرَةٌ) (with nadrata in the nominative case). (٢٥) يُسْقَوْنَ مِنْ رَحِيقٍ ** (25) They will be served with a sealed pure wine, A pure drink. مَخْتُومٍ. (٢٦) خِتامُهُ مِسْكٌ ** (26) whose seal is musk. It was said that its vessels are sealed with musk instead of clay, and perhaps it is a representation of its preciousness. And al-Qummi said: It is water; when the believer drinks it, he finds the scent of musk in it. I say: Perhaps He meant by it that he finds it (the scent) at the end of his drink. And it was recited (خَاتَمُهُ) with a fathah on the tā’, meaning: that with which it is sealed. **وَفِي ذلِكَ فَلْيَتَنافَسِ الْمُتَنافِسُونَ ** For this let the competitors compete. Let the desirous ones desire.

(٢٧) وَمِزاجُهُ مِنْ تَسْنِيمٍ (27) and its mixture is of Tasnīm, It is a proper name (علم) for a specific spring (عين بعينها). It was named Tasnīm due to the elevation of its location or the loftiness of its drink. It was said that it is the verbal noun (مصدر) of سَنَّمَهُ when He raised it, because it is the loftiest drink of the people of Paradise, or because it comes to them from above. And al-Qummi said: It is the noblest drink of the people of Paradise, which comes to them from on high, flowing down (يسنم) upon them in their dwellings.

** عَيْناً يَشْرَبُ بِهَا الْمُقَرَّبُونَ ** (28) a spring from which those brought near [to Allah] drink.

He said: They are the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). Allah says: "The foremost, the foremost, they are the ones brought near [to Him]." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and Khadijah, and Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام, and their descendants (ذُرِّيَّاتُهُمْ) will join them. Allah says: أَلْحَقْنا بِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ ** (We will make their descendants join them). And the ones brought near (الْمُقَرَّبُونَ) drink from Tasnīm purely (صَرْفاً), while the rest of the believers drink it mixed (مَمْزُوجاً). It was said that they only drink it purely (صَرْفاً) because they were not preoccupied with anything other than Allah.

(٢٩) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا كَانُوا مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يَضْحَكُونَ ** (29) Indeed, the guilty used to laugh at those who had faith. They mock. (٣٠) وَإِذا مَرُّوا بِهِمْ يَتَغامَزُونَ ** (30) And when they passed by them, they would wink at one another. Some of them would wink at others and point with their eyes. (٣١) وَإِذَا انْقَلَبُوا إِلى أَهْلِهِمُ انْقَلَبُوا فَكِهِينَ ** (31) And when they returned to their folks, they would return exulting. Taking pleasure in mocking them. And it was read (فَكِهِينَ). Al-Qummi said: They mock. Al-Qummi said: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا ** refers to the First (الأوّل) and the Second (الثاني) and those who followed them, winking at the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) until the end of the Surah. And in al-Majma': It was said that it was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). That was because he was among a group of Muslims who came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and the hypocrites mocked them, laughed, and winked at one another. Then they returned to their companions and said: "Today we saw al-Asla' (the bald one), and we laughed at him." So the verses were revealed before Ali and his companions reached the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). And from Ibn Abbas: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا ** refers to the hypocrites of Quraysh, and ** الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** refers to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him).

(٣٢) وَإِذا رَأَوْهُمْ قالُوا إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ لَضالُّونَ ** (32) and when they saw them, they would say, 'These are indeed astray.' And when they saw the believers, they attributed error (الضلال) to them. (٣٣) وَما أُرْسِلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ حافِظِينَ ** (33) Yet they were not sent to be guardians over them. Over the believers, ** حافِظِينَ ** (guardians) who preserve their deeds for them and bear witness to their guidance (رشدهم) or their error (ضلالتهم). (٣٤)** فَالْيَوْمَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنَ الْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ ** (34) So today the faithful will laugh at the faithless, When they see them abased, shackled in the Fire. And it is narrated: that a gate to Paradise will be opened for them, and it will be said to them, "Go out to it." But when they reach it, it will be closed before them, and the believers will laugh at them.

(٣٥) عَلَى الْأَرائِكِ يَنْظُرُونَ. (35) upon couches, looking on. (٣٦) هَلْ ثُوِّبَ الْكُفَّارُ ** Have they been rewarded ما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ.** (36) Have the faithless been requited for what they used to do?

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Wayl lil-Mutaffifin (ويل للمطفّفين) in the obligatory prayer (الفريضة), He grants him safety (الأمن) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) from the Fire (النّار). It (the Fire) will not see him, nor will he see it. He will not pass over the bridge of Hell (جسر جهنّم), nor will he be called to account on the Day of Resurrection, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).

سُورَة انْشَقَّتْ

And it is called Sūrat al-Inshiqāq. It is Makkan, and the number of its verses is twenty-three verses.

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
